AboutPage.title: The ideal architect, wherever you are
AboutPage.intro1: At Hauss Paris, we are not just a simple connection agency. We are the partner who helps you take the step to start your project, giving you access to the best architects, here and elsewhere.
AboutPage.intro2: Launching an architecture project is an important decision, often hindered by the complexity of finding the right contact. We exist to simplify this crucial step.
AboutPage.intro3: Our promise is twofold: to offer you high-level alternatives among the best architects of the moment, and to facilitate every step of the connection. Whether your project is located in Paris or internationally, or you wish to engage a foreign architect for a realization in France, we orchestrate this meeting to guarantee the success of your endeavor.

metadata.title: Our Process - Hauss Paris
metadata.description: Discover our 3-step methodology: Discovery, Design and Delivery. A structured approach to successfully carry out your interior design project.

discover.title: Definition
discover.paragraph1: Starting an architecture project can be intimidating. Our first mission is to help you take the step. We transform your ideas, sometimes vague, into a concrete and achievable project.
discover.paragraph2: We take the time to analyze your deep needs, your lifestyle and your ambitions. Whether it's a residence in Paris or an international project, we structure your request so that it is perfectly understandable by professionals.
discover.paragraph3: This crucial step allows us to define a clear specification, an essential foundation for approaching the right contacts.

build.title: Selection
build.paragraph1: Once the project is defined, we activate our network. Our strength is to offer you alternatives. We don't impose a single choice, but present you with a restricted selection of the best profiles.
build.paragraph2: This selection is made among the top 1% of architects, in Paris as well as internationally. We seek the perfect match: creative talent, technical expertise and human compatibility.
build.paragraph3: Do you already have a name in mind? An architect you admire but who seems inaccessible? We take care of connecting you with the architects you have chosen, facilitating the approach and negotiation.

deliver.title: Launch
deliver.paragraph1: Our role doesn't stop at the recommendation. We are here to facilitate the connection. We organize meetings, help with reading proposals and secure the contractual framework.
deliver.paragraph2: Whether your project is local or we are managing an international collaboration, we act as a trusted third party. We remove language and cultural barriers so that only the project matters.
deliver.paragraph3: By choosing Hauss Paris, you start your project with the assurance of being surrounded by the best, ready to see your ambitions take shape without unnecessary friction.

metadata.title: Commercial Architecture Paris | Restaurant, Hotel, Boutique
metadata.description: Design your commercial space in Paris with a specialized retail architect. Restaurant, hotel, boutique. ERP standards respected, turnkey project. Free quote.

section1.title: Your commercial project in expert hands
section1.paragraph1: Opening a restaurant, designing a boutique, transforming a space into a hotel: each commercial project in Paris requires specific expertise. Between safety standards, accessibility constraints and brand identity creation, challenges are numerous.
section1.paragraph2: Hauss Paris connects you with architects and interior designers specialized in commercial design. Retail design, hospitality, restaurants: our network includes professionals experienced in each sector.
section1.paragraph3: Whether you're an entrepreneur, franchisee, investor or professional, we find the right architect for your project.

section2.title: Why hire a specialized retail architect?
section2.paragraph1: Commercial design cannot be improvised. Beyond aesthetics, it's about creating a space that complies with regulations, is functional for your activity and attractive to your customers.
section2.paragraph2: In Paris, every public establishment (ERP) must meet strict standards: fire safety, accessibility, kitchen extraction, sound insulation. A specialized architect knows these constraints and designs a compliant project from the start.
section2.paragraph3: A well-designed business generates more revenue. Layout influences customer flow, product presentation, time spent on premises.

section3.title: Restaurants and cafés in Paris
section3.paragraph1: From neighborhood bistro to gastronomic restaurant, our architects design spaces that tell a story. They master restaurant-specific constraints: kitchen/dining flow, extraction, hygiene standards, acoustics.
section3.paragraph2: Each restaurant project is unique: culinary concept, desired ambiance, number of seats, type of cuisine. The architect adapts the space to your vision while respecting strict ERP standards.
section3.paragraph3: From intimate 30 m² coffee shops to 150-seat restaurants, we support all formats. The goal: create a place where your customers will want to return.

section4.title: Retail shops and stores
section4.paragraph1: Designing a shop is not just about installing furniture. It's about creating a customer journey, showcasing your products, expressing your brand identity. Our retail architects master these challenges.
section4.paragraph2: From independent concept stores to international franchises, each shop has its constraints: attractive storefront, optimized storage areas, comfortable fitting rooms, lighting that enhances products.
section4.paragraph3: We work with fashion boutiques, concept stores, decoration shops, bookstores. The goal: turn visits into sales, create a memorable experience.

section5.title: Hotels and hospitality spaces
section5.paragraph1: Creating or renovating a hotel requires specific expertise: strict regulations, specific safety standards, surface optimization per room, creating varied ambiances. Our specialized architects master these constraints.
section5.paragraph2: From budget hotels to boutique hotels, each category has its standards: minimum surface per room, mandatory equipment, accessibility, acoustic comfort. The architect designs compliant and attractive spaces.
section5.paragraph3: Beyond rooms, common areas make the difference: welcoming lobby, bright breakfast room, cozy bar. We create hotels where travelers feel at home.

section6.title: Medical and paramedical offices
section6.paragraph1: A medical, dental or paramedical office must meet strict hygiene standards, offer confidentiality and comfort to patients, optimize the care pathway. Our architects know these requirements.
section6.paragraph2: Each specialty has its needs: sterilization room for dentists, separate waiting area for general practitioners, physiotherapy space with accessibility. The architect adapts the layout to your practice.
section6.paragraph3: Ambiance matters too: a welcoming medical office reassures patients, improves their experience, reflects your professionalism. We create care spaces that are both functional and calming.

section7.title: Offices and coworking spaces
section7.paragraph1: Workspaces are evolving: fewer closed offices, more collaborative zones, need for flexibility. Our architects design modern offices, adapted to new ways of working.
section7.paragraph2: Good office design improves productivity, fosters collaboration, attracts talent. Open space, meeting rooms, breakout areas, phone booths: everything is designed for team well-being.
section7.paragraph3: From small consulting firms to large coworking spaces, we adapt the layout to your company culture and operational needs.

section8.title: Showrooms and event spaces
section8.paragraph1: Showrooms, pop-up stores, event spaces: these ephemeral or permanent venues must leave a lasting impression. Our architects create scenographic spaces that showcase your products or brand.
section8.paragraph2: Flexibility is key: modular furniture, adjustable lighting, removable partitions. The space must be able to evolve according to collections, events, seasons.
section8.paragraph3: From automotive showrooms to luxury pop-up stores, we design spaces that create events and strengthen your brand image.

section9.title: ERP norms and regulations
section9.paragraph1: All Parisian businesses are ERP (Public Access Establishments) and must comply with strict standards: fire safety (emergency exits, extinguishers, safety lighting), accessibility (ramps, elevators, adapted toilets), ventilation (extraction for professional kitchens).
section9.paragraph2: These standards are not optional: before any opening, the town hall and safety commission inspect the premises. If the project is not compliant, opening is refused. An experienced architect integrates these constraints from the design stage.
section9.paragraph3: Beyond compliance, a good architect transforms constraints into assets: an emergency exit can become a terrace, an accessibility ramp a design element. We design spaces that are both compliant and beautiful.

section10.title: Turnkey project management
section10.paragraph1: Opening a business is already an entrepreneurial challenge. You don't have time to chase contractors, manage delays, validate a thousand technical details. That's why we offer complete project management.
section10.paragraph2: The architect becomes your conductor: they coordinate all trades (electrician, plumber, carpenter, painter), manage the schedule, control quality, solve unforeseen issues. You validate major decisions, they handle execution.
section10.paragraph3: Result: your business opens on schedule, within budget, compliant with standards. You focus on your business, the architect handles the rest.

section11.title: Commercial architecture in Paris and Île-de-France
section11.paragraph1: Paris and its region concentrate unique commercial density: each neighborhood has its identity, codes, expectations. From trendy Marais to chic Champs-Élysées, from creative 11th to bourgeois 16th, the architect adapts the project to the territory.
section11.paragraph2: Our network covers Paris city center and all of Île-de-France: Neuilly, Boulogne, Levallois for business districts, Montreuil and Saint-Denis for creatives, Versailles and Saint-Germain-en-Laye for upscale residential.
section11.paragraph3: Whether you're opening a business in the city center or suburbs, we find the architect who knows your sector, understands your target clientele, masters local constraints.

q1.question: How long does it take to fit out a commercial space in Paris?
q1.answer: Allow 2 to 3 months for a simple project (refresh, light boutique), 4 to 6 months for a restaurant or major restructuring, 6 to 12 months for a hotel. Timeframes include design, administrative procedures and construction.

q2.question: Can you support me before finding the space?
q2.answer: Yes. The architect can accompany you during space visits to evaluate potential: technical feasibility, construction estimate, hidden constraints. This pre-purchase consultation service avoids bad surprises.

q3.question: I don't own the walls. Is that a problem?
q3.answer: No. Most commercial spaces are leased. The architect designs a fit-out adapted to your lease: work authorized by owner, recoverable elements at lease end, etc.

q4.question: Do you handle administrative procedures (ERP, licenses)?
q4.answer: Yes, it's included in the architect's mission. They compile files, submit applications and represent you to administrations if necessary.

q5.question: I'm opening a franchise. Can you adapt the concept to the space?
q5.answer: Absolutely. The architect respects the franchisor's specifications while adapting to Paris space constraints. They dialogue with the brand to validate necessary adaptations.

q6.question: I'm an investor and not on site. How does it work?
q6.answer: We're used to supporting investors who cannot be present at every step. The architect manages the project autonomously, keeps you informed via videoconference and reports, and solicits you only for key validations.

q7.question: What's the minimum budget for a commercial fit-out project?
q7.answer: Below 30,000 € of construction, an architect's involvement is rarely economically justified. For smaller projects, we can refer you to a one-time consultation service.

q8.question: Do you work outside Paris?
q8.answer: Yes. Our network covers Paris and all Île-de-France: Boulogne, Neuilly, Saint-Germain-en-Laye, Versailles, and departments 92, 93, 94, 78, 91, 77, 95.

metadata.title: Privacy Policy - Hauss Paris
metadata.description: Learn how Hauss Paris collects, uses, and protects your personal information when you use our interior designer matching service.

section1.title: 1. Information We Collect
section1.paragraph1: We collect information that you provide directly to us when using our interior designer matching service, including: your name, email address, phone number, project details (surface area, budget, address, timeline), and any messages or communications you send to us through our contact forms. This information allows us to match you with appropriate interior designers from our curated network in Paris and internationally.

section2.title: 2. How We Use Your Information
section2.paragraph1: We use the information we collect to: match you with appropriate interior designers based on your project requirements and preferences, communicate with you about your project and the matching process, respond to your inquiries and requests, send you confirmation emails and project updates, improve our services and user experience, and comply with our legal obligations under French and European law.

section3.title: 3. Information Sharing
section3.paragraph1: We do not sell your personal information to third parties. We may share your information with: interior designers in our network for matching purposes (only designers who match your project criteria will receive your information), service providers who assist in our operations (such as email delivery services and analytics platforms), and legal authorities when required by law or to protect our rights.
section3.paragraph2: All interior designers in our network are bound by confidentiality agreements and must comply with GDPR regulations when handling your personal data.

section4.title: 4. Data Security
section4.paragraph1: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. These measures include encryption, secure servers, and regular security audits. However, no method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security. We maintain industry-standard security practices and continuously update our protocols to protect your data.

section5.title: 5. Your Rights
section5.paragraph1: Under GDPR and French data protection law, you have the right to: access the personal information we hold about you, request correction of inaccurate or incomplete information, request deletion of your information (subject to legal obligations), object to processing of your information, request restriction of processing, data portability, and withdraw consent at any time. To exercise these rights, contact us at contact@haussparis.com with your request and proof of identity.

section6.title: 6. Cookies and Tracking
section6.paragraph1: We use Google Analytics to understand how visitors use our website and to improve user experience. This involves cookies and similar tracking technologies that collect information about your browsing behavior. You can control cookies through your browser settings and opt-out of Google Analytics tracking. For more information about Google Analytics and how to opt-out, visit Google's privacy policy and the Google Analytics opt-out browser add-on.

section7.title: 7. Third-Party Links
section7.paragraph1: Our website may contain links to third-party websites, including the portfolios and websites of interior designers in our network. We are not responsible for the privacy practices of these external sites. We encourage you to review their privacy policies before providing any personal information to them.

section8.title: 8. Children's Privacy
section8.paragraph1: Our services are not directed to individuals under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If we become aware that we have collected personal information from a child under 18, we will take steps to delete such information promptly.

section9.title: 9. Changes to This Policy
section9.paragraph1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or legal requirements. We will notify you of any material changes by posting the new policy on this page and updating the 'Last Updated' date at the top. We encourage you to review this policy periodically. Your continued use of our services after changes are posted constitutes your acceptance of the updated policy.

ArchitecteInterieurParisService.title: Interior Designer in Paris: Your Renovation Project Supported from A to Z
ArchitecteInterieurParisService.intro: Looking to renovate an apartment in Paris but don't know where to start? Hauss Paris simplifies this process by connecting you with carefully selected Parisian interior designers.

section1.title: Your Parisian Project in Good Hands
section1.paragraph1: Whether you live in Paris, abroad, or are investing in the French capital, finding the right interior designer can be complex. Our mission: to enable you to complete your renovation project with complete peace of mind, wherever you are in the world.
section1.paragraph2: Since our inception, we have supported an international clientele with their renovation projects in Paris. Expatriates, investors, second home owners: we understand your specific constraints and provide concrete solutions.

section2.title: Why Hire an Interior Designer in Paris?
section2.paragraph1: Renovating a Parisian apartment presents unique challenges. Between the constraints of old buildings, co-ownership regulations, and the specificities of the local market, professional support makes all the difference.
section2.paragraph2: An interior designer knows how to exploit your property's potential: custom storage creation, intelligent room redistribution, natural light maximization. They preserve Parisian character while modernizing your interior.

section3.title: Our Network of Interior Designers in Paris
section3.paragraph1: At Hauss Paris, we don't work with just anyone. Each interior designer in our network has been selected according to strict criteria: recognized diploma, minimum 5 years of experience in Paris, up-to-date insurance, verified references.
section3.paragraph2: We don't offer you a random list of designers. After understanding your project, tastes, and constraints, we identify the professional best suited to your situation.

section4.title: How Does Your Project Work?
section4.paragraph1: Our support is structured in four clear stages. First, a free initial consultation of 30 to 45 minutes to understand your needs. Then, we present you with 1 to 3 interior designers selected for your project.
section4.paragraph2: The interior designer then takes action with study and design: survey, layout proposals, material selection, budget estimation. Finally, during the works, they coordinate interventions and keep you regularly informed.

sectionIntro.title: Personalized Service for Your Parisian Project
sectionIntro.description: From initial consultation to project completion, we support you at every stage with transparency and professionalism.

q1.question: I live abroad, how can I manage my renovation project in Paris?
q1.answer: This is our specialty. We organize exchanges via video conference, share documents online, and ensure regular reporting with construction site photos. Several of our clients have conducted their project without traveling to Paris once during the works.

q2.question: Do you speak English or Italian?
q2.answer: Yes. Our team and several designers in our network are bilingual or trilingual. We can conduct your entire project in English or Italian.

q3.question: How do you select the interior designers in your network?
q3.answer: We verify diplomas, insurance, references, and meet each professional before integrating them into our network. We monitor client satisfaction to ensure consistent quality.

q4.question: What is the minimum budget to hire an interior designer?
q4.answer: There is no imposed minimum budget. In practice, an interior designer's intervention is fully justified from €30,000 of works. Below that, a one-time consultation service may suffice.

q5.question: How long does an apartment renovation in Paris take?
q5.answer: Allow 2 to 3 months for partial renovation, 4 to 6 months for complete renovation. Timelines depend on the scope of works, craftsmen availability, and administrative authorizations.

q6.question: Do you work outside of Paris?
q6.answer: Yes. Our network covers Paris proper and all of Île-de-France: Neuilly, Boulogne, Saint-Cloud, Versailles, and departments 92, 93, 94, 78, 91, 77, 95.

q7.question: What happens if I'm not satisfied with the proposed designer?
q7.answer: You are free to refuse our proposal and request another profile. The initial consultation with Hauss Paris is free and without obligation.

section1.title: Interior Decoration: A Fast and Accessible Transformation
section1.paragraph1: Unlike an interior designer who works on the structure (partitions, electricity, plumbing), the decorator works exclusively on dressing your space: furniture selection, color palette, textiles, lighting and decorative objects.
section1.paragraph2: This approach offers considerable advantages: no construction permits required, no contractor coordination, controlled budget between €5,000 and €50,000, and a project completed in 3 to 8 weeks versus several months for a full renovation.

section2.title: For Whom? Ideal Situations for a Decorator
section2.paragraph1: Just bought an apartment in good condition but with outdated style? Preparing your property for seasonal rental or Airbnb? You're an investor looking to enhance a property before sale? An interior decorator is the optimal solution.
section2.paragraph2: Particularly suited for expats and remote owners, this service enables 100% remote management with online furniture selection, coordinated delivery and supervised installation, all without requiring your physical presence.

q1.question: What's the difference between decorator and interior designer?
q1.answer: The decorator works only on dressing (furniture, colors, accessories) without touching the structure. The interior designer can modify partitions, redo electricity and plumbing. Budget and timelines are very different: €5-50k in 3-8 weeks for decorator, €30-200k+ in 3-6 months for designer.

q2.question: Can I manage my decoration project from abroad?
q2.answer: Absolutely. It's actually one of our main use cases. Everything is done remotely: furniture selection by video call or email, coordinated deliveries, installation supervised by decorator. You receive photos at each stage and get back a ready-to-live apartment.

q3.question: What's the minimum budget for hiring a decorator?
q3.answer: From €5,000 (furniture + fees), you can refresh a space with new textiles, accessories and some key furniture pieces. For complete furnishing of a studio or 1-bedroom, budget €15,000 to €25,000.

q4.question: How long does a decoration project take?
q4.answer: From 3 to 8 weeks depending on scope: 3-4 weeks for partial refresh, 6-8 weeks for complete furnishing. Timelines depend mainly on furniture delivery times (typically 2-6 weeks).

q5.question: Can the decorator help with an Airbnb?
q5.answer: Yes, several decorators in our network specialize in short-term rentals. They know the visual codes that work on Airbnb, favor robust materials and optimize quality-price ratio to maximize your profitability.

q6.question: How is the decorator compensated?
q6.answer: Either a flat fee for the decor concept (€800-1500), or a percentage of furniture budget for full service (15-25%). Some decorators combine both: flat fee for design + reduced percentage on sourcing. Everything is transparent from the quote.

q7.question: Can I provide my own furniture and only pay for advice?
q7.answer: Yes, you can opt for an "advice only" service: the decorator provides mood boards, shopping list with references and layout plan. You then manage orders and installation yourself. Fee: €800 to €1,500 depending on surface.

q8.question: Do you work outside Paris?
q8.answer: Yes. Our network covers Paris proper and all Île-de-France: Neuilly, Boulogne, Versailles, Saint-Cloud and departments 92, 93, 94, 78, 91, 77, 95.

FAQPage.title: Frequently Asked Questions About Interior Design
FAQPage.intro: Have questions about your renovation or interior design project in Paris? Find here the answers to our clients' most frequently asked questions.

q1.question: How much does an interior designer cost in Paris?
q1.answer: Interior designer fees in Paris vary by project type. For a one-time consultation, expect €150-300/hour. For a complete project, fees typically represent 8-15% of construction costs, ranging from €5,000 to €50,000 depending on scope. Hauss Paris helps you find a professional suited to your budget.

q2.question: What's the difference between an interior designer and a decorator?
q2.answer: An interior designer can modify your space's structure (partitions, plumbing, electrical) and coordinate major construction. A decorator works on styling: furniture selection, colors, textiles, accessories. For a complete renovation, choose an interior designer. For a refresh without construction, a decorator suffices.

q3.question: Can I manage my renovation project from abroad?
q3.answer: Absolutely, this is actually our specialty at Hauss Paris. We support many international clients (expats, investors, second-home owners). The designer manages everything on-site: surveys, construction monitoring, deliveries. You receive regular updates with photos and validate key stages remotely.

q4.question: How do I choose an interior designer in Paris?
q4.answer: Check their diploma (CFAI, ENSAD, recognized school), references, and style. Ask for examples of projects similar to yours. Ensure they have professional liability insurance. Hauss Paris verifies all these points before recommending a designer and presents you with 1-3 profiles suited to your project.

q5.question: What budget should I plan for an apartment renovation in Paris?
q5.answer: Budget depends on scope: simple refresh (€500-800/sqm), partial renovation (€1,000-1,500/sqm), complete renovation (€1,500-2,500/sqm), high-end renovation (€2,500-4,000/sqm). These prices include construction but not designer fees (8-15% extra) or furniture.

q6.question: Are designer fees negotiable?
q6.answer: Designers in our network offer transparent pricing from the first contact. Their billing method (flat fee, percentage, or hourly rate) is adapted to your project's nature. We help you compare quotes and choose the most relevant formula.

q7.question: What are the hidden costs of a renovation?
q7.answer: Plan for a 10-15% margin for unexpected issues: discoveries during demolition (old plumbing, non-compliant electrical), supplier delays, mid-project changes. A good designer anticipates these risks and advises you on items to secure.

q8.question: How long does an apartment renovation take in Paris?
q8.answer: Plan 2-3 months for a partial renovation (kitchen, bathroom), 4-6 months for a complete renovation of an 80-120 sqm apartment. Timelines include the study phase (1-2 months), condo board approvals (1-2 months), and actual construction (2-4 months).

q9.question: Do I need a building permit to renovate an apartment in Paris?
q9.answer: A building permit is rarely needed for interior renovation. However, you must obtain condo board approval for work affecting common areas or modifying the exterior appearance. Some listed buildings have additional constraints that your designer will manage.

q10.question: How does a project work with Hauss Paris?
q10.answer: 1) Free 30-min consultation to understand your project. 2) We select 1-3 suited designers. 3) You meet the candidates and choose. 4) The designer handles your project (study, construction, furnishing). Our matching service is free for individuals.

q11.question: Do you work on commercial projects?
q11.answer: Yes, our network includes designers specialized in commercial spaces: shops, restaurants, hotels, offices. They know ERP standards, accessibility requirements, and tight retail deadlines. Contact us with your specifications.

q12.question: Do you offer home staging services?
q12.answer: Yes, several decorators in our network specialize in home staging for sale or rental. They optimize your property's presentation to accelerate transactions and maximize price. Service particularly useful for investors.

q13.question: Do you work outside Paris?
q13.answer: Our network covers central Paris and all Île-de-France: Neuilly, Boulogne, Versailles, Saint-Germain-en-Laye, and departments 92, 93, 94, 78. For projects in provinces or abroad, contact us to explore possibilities.
